title: Note port publishing needs in pods for create/run
body:
Add notes to the podman-create and podman-run man pages
to note that ports do not need to be published and should not
be, for containers that will be part of a pod.

Addresses: #6769

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

IMO, we have to error on the `podman create` command. I've seen some people run into it and not realize the error.

This should not work:
```bash
$ podman pod create --name t1
$ podman create --pod t1 -p 8080:80 nginx
```
@Luap99, good thought.  I created #7373 and will chase that down later today.
I've repushed with @mheon's change, plus have added a section to the troubleshooting guide.
@TomSweeneyRedHat @Luap99 what is going on with this PR?
LGTM, my concern got addressed in a different PR
